office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: The open-seat field includes nonprofit leader Katy Cornell, student leader Randy Phillips, educator Renee Hernandez Greenfield, and small-business owner Tedd Wetherbee.

candidates.summary: Cornell is a nonprofit leader and pastor emphasizing affordability, safety, parental rights, small businesses, and fiscal restraint.
highlights: Emphasizes public safety, parental rights, and fiscal restraint [S2].

spending.basis: Fiscal restraint supplies an audit-and-restrain direction without a detailed mechanism.

experience.basis: She brings nonprofit leadership but no documented elected or legislative record.

candidates.summary: Phillips is a college student-body and student-association leader emphasizing education affordability, workforce training, housing, representation, and accountability.
highlights: Brings student-government leadership and emphasizes accountability [S2].

spending.basis: Government accountability supplies an oversight direction without a detailed spending proposal.

experience.basis: He has institutional student-governance leadership but no documented elected public-office record.

candidates.summary: Hernandez Greenfield is an educator and community advocate emphasizing education, housing, health-care access, worker protections, reproductive rights, and ferries.
highlights: Supports reproductive rights and public education [S2].

experience.basis: She brings education, advocacy, and party-organization experience but no documented elected record.

candidates.summary: Wetherbee is a small-business owner emphasizing affordability, transportation, reproductive rights, and environmental protection.
highlights: Supports reproductive rights and environmental protection [S2].

experience.basis: He brings business and advocacy experience but no documented public-office record.
